prompt: A media buyer named Sam (young man with short dark hair and glasses) sits at a desk with four monitors, each showing a completely different advertising dashboard. His desk is covered in spreadsheets and budget documents. Each screen has different metrics, different UI layouts, different terminology. He looks tired and frustrated. A clock on the wall shows late afternoon. Graphic novel panel style.

Sam types a single document on his laptop — a creative brief with a glowing aura. The brief sends out clean teal rays that reach three different friendly robots standing at podiums. Each robot represents a different publisher/sales agent. They each receive the same brief and start examining it with interest. The rays form clean parallel lines showing one brief going to many destinations. Graphic novel panel style.

The three robots from the previous panel each present their product offerings back to Sam. Each robot holds up a card showing what they can offer — one shows video inventory (a TV icon), one shows display placements (banner rectangles), one shows audio spots (a speaker icon). Sam reviews them side by side on a single clean screen where all three are shown in the same format, easily comparable. He looks pleased. Graphic novel panel style.

A split panel. Left side: creative format templates float in the air like puzzle pieces — a 300x250 banner shape, a 16:9 video frame, a social post card. Right side: actual creative assets (a landscape photo, headline text, a logo) snap into these templates with satisfying click lines showing perfect fit. A small robot assistant helps match assets to formats. Graphic novel panel style.

Sam presses a single button on his laptop and a campaign blueprint materializes above his desk as a holographic projection. The blueprint shows the campaign structure: at the top is the campaign name, below are three branches (tactics), each connecting to their matched creative and target audience. Budget amounts flow along the branches like energy. The whole structure glows teal. Graphic novel panel style.

The campaign blueprint from the previous panel passes through a checkpoint staffed by a guardian robot with a shield emblem. The robot scans the campaign with a teal beam. Three checkmark indicators appear: budget within limits (checkmark), brand safety verified (checkmark), targeting compliant (checkmark). The campaign gets a green seal of approval stamp. A human supervisor watches from a glass office above, nodding approvingly. Graphic novel panel style.

Sam relaxes at his desk, now with a single clean dashboard on one large monitor. The dashboard shows campaign performance — bar charts rising, impression counters ticking up, a map showing ad delivery across regions. Small icons show ads running on different channels simultaneously. The three publisher robots from earlier send real-time updates that stream into the unified view. Sam smiles, coffee in hand. Graphic novel panel style.
